$NBIS signs a 5-year AI infrastructure deal with $META worth up to $27B.

The agreement includes $12B of dedicated capacity starting in 2027 plus a commitment from Meta to purchase up to $15B of additional compute across future Nebius clusters.

THE BIGGEST AI EVENT OF THE YEAR IS HERE

Jensen Huang is hours away from his keynote at $NVDA GTC 2026.

Here’s what Daniel & Shay expect to be revealed on stage and why Nvidia may be positioning itself to dominate the next phase of AI.

It never stops surprising me how nobody wants a stock on the pullback but everyone wants it ~35% higher. 

Now after a $2B $NVDA investment & $27B $META deal, everyone suddenly wants $NBIS exposure.

The headline that made the thesis obvious to everyone who hadn’t done the work.
At this point $SOFI might need to announce an AI data center deal.
$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang saw the AI era coming in 2010 before $AAPL even launched Siri.

They added ~61K $ETH last week bringing their total to ~4.53M.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says the U.S. is allowing Iranian oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

The goal is to avoid disrupting global oil flows through one of the world’s most critical shipping lanes.

$CRWV and Cerebras are building a 300-MW AI data center in Canada with the first phase expected online in H1 2027.

The facility will dedicate 160 MW to Cerebras systems and 140 MW to $NVDA GPU infrastructure via CoreWeave.

$NBIS is up ~14% after announcing a $27B AI infrastructure deal with $META.

Nebius is positioning itself as a cloud utility for the Agentic AI era.

$SNDK a great example of relative strength from Friday.  Trim and trail.  See how much of today’s gap up holds.

📺 DON’T RUSH TO SHORT TODAY’S MARKET OPEN — BUT THIS ISN’T THE MARKET BOTTOM YET

Many traders expected a sharp selloff after Friday’s weak close to start this week. Instead, the market points to opening stronger, creating a situation where aggressively shorting the open could be risky.

Furthermore, with the short-term oscillator near -50, the market was already stretched, increasing the odds of a bounce rather than an immediate breakdown.

The key question now is whether the early strength will hold or fade as the day progresses.

I am watching key $SPY ($666 → $669 → Friday’s high) and $QQQ ($591 support → $597 resistance → Friday’s rejection zone) levels while also keeping an eye on upcoming catalysts, including the Fed meeting this week and an $NVDA event today.

These levels will likely determine whether the market continues to grind higher or rolls over again.

#Crudeoil remaining relatively stable near $100 may also allow equities some room to bounce. $USO

However, despite the possible bounce, the market is still only about 5% off its highs, which does not qualify as true panic or capitulation.

Because of that, it is far too early to call a durable market bottom, and deeper downside levels may still be tested before a real “blood in the streets” appears.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

The oscillator was out at -51, $SPX is about 5% off highs, and now we watch if opening strength holds or fades with 6710–6740 as key resistance. Also keep an eye on China talk, Jensen’s $NVDA keynote, and #FOMC Wednesday.

Copper miners ($COPX) down 4.2% in a single session.

When Dr. Copper rolls over while oil rips higher, the market is pricing in stagflation — rising costs with falling demand.

Classic late-cycle.

AAII bearish sentiment at 46.4%.

Historically a contrarian buy signal. But 2001 and 2008 proved sentiment can stay extreme when fundamentals deteriorate.

Which analog applies? That's the trade.

BlackRock restricted withdrawals on its $26B private credit fund. Blackstone got $3.8B in redemption requests.

JP Morgan cut lending to private debt funds.

When the exits close, the panic starts.

Big Tech committed $660-690B in AI capex for 2026. Nearly double 2025.

Meanwhile the $SPY is at its lows and payrolls went negative.

Capex doesn't care about your recession.

Silver crashed 5% while gold barely moved.

When the gold-silver ratio blows out, it's a recession signal — not a metals trade.

Watch $SLV relative to $GLD. It's screaming.

Private credit default rate just hit 5.8%.

Apollo down 41%. Blackstone down 46%. Blue Owl down 66% from peak.

$265 billion in market cap erased. This is not a headline. It's a crisis.

The Fed meets this week. 97% chance they hold.

Can't cut because of oil. Can't hike because of jobs. Can't do nothing because the economy is deteriorating.

$TLT knows.

$SPY at 2026 lows. Three straight weeks of losses.

The "buy the dip" crowd is slowly becoming the "where's the bottom" crowd.

Sentiment shifts are silent until they're deafening.

Brent just breached $106. Three weeks into the Hormuz blockade and the only plan is "when it's militarily possible."

$WTI is the macro variable that breaks everything else.
HY credit spreads widened from 264 bps in January to 312 bps by late February. Still well below the long-term average of 520 bps, but the direction matters.
Was I right all along that we’re fucked and it just took longer to materialize just how fucked?

Iran's supreme leader says the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed.

Tankers are burning near Basra.

The market is treating this like a temporary disruption. The IEA is treating it like a structural one.

Someone is wrong.

Private credit is loaded with software loans.

AI is disrupting the same software companies backing those loans.

This is circular risk that almost nobody is stress-testing.

Gold up. Oil up. Bonds down. Stocks wobbling. Dollar flat.

When everything moves at once, the market is repricing the entire macro regime.

That's not a correction. That's a reset.
